sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Depression
sources.population: Adults presenting with suspected or established depressive symptoms; emergency, severe, psychotic, bipolar, or safeguarding presentations require escalation.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth depression documentation and risk screening; treatment, diagnosis, and local emergency pathways remain clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — depressive features and structured assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports low mood, loss of interest, sleep, appetite, energy, concentration, guilt or worthlessness, duration, function, previous episodes and treatment, medical and medication history, stressors, social context, comorbidity, mania or hypomania, substance use, family history, and support assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — risk, mental-status examination, and selected investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician assessment of suicide, self-harm, harm or neglect, protective and support factors, mental status, cognition, insight, substance use, medical contributors, medication history, thyroid testing, and selected blood testing when indicated.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists suicidal ideation or attempt, risk of harm or neglect, moderate or severe depression, and psychotic or bipolar features as escalation concerns.

exact_sections.heading: Differential diagnosis — adjustment disorder with depressed mood
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes depressed mood in response to an identifiable psychosocial stressor and supports documenting single, multiple, recurrent, or continuing social and occupational stressors.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management, monitoring, and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented supportive discussion, questions, safety planning, treatment or medication decisions, monitoring, follow-up, and referral without autonomous treatment selection or doses.

sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia
sources.population: Adults with difficulty initiating or maintaining sleep or early waking with daytime impact.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth insomnia history, red flags, causes, and clinician-led management; fixed follow-up intervals and autonomous medication choices are not supported.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 5–7 — sleep history and daytime consequences
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports sleep initiation or maintenance difficulty, early waking, non-restorative sleep, adequate opportunity, fatigue, concentration, mood, irritability, daytime sleepiness, motivation, duration, stress, and functional impact.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — red flags and evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sleep attacks, apnoea or gasping, unstable cardiopulmonary disease, stroke, injury during sleep, sleepwalking, excessive daytime sleepiness, substance misuse, depression, anxiety, and significant functional impact; supports sleep-diary and targeted evaluation.

exact_sections.heading: Section 7 — substances, medicines, comorbidity, environment, and selected tests
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

exact_sections.heading: Sections 8–9 — clinician-led management and referral
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented discussion of sleep routine or hygiene, lifestyle context, psychological or medication plans, reassessment, questions, monitoring, and referral without autonomous advice or doses.
